notes.note: The unnamed "he" is Caesar, detained in Egypt after the Alexandrian War of 48–47 BC; his return westward was the event on which Cicero's release from Brundisium depended. Cicero habitually avoids naming Caesar in these anxious letters.
"Cicero" here is young Quintus, Cicero's nephew, whom he had thought to send to Caesar to plead the family's case but now holds back.

notes.anchor: The twelfth day before the Kalends of Quintilis
notes.note: Quintilis was the old Roman name for the month later renamed July in Caesar's honour; the twelfth day before its Kalends (first) is 20 June. Cicero still uses the pre-Julian month-name.
